title: Iterator Traversal Printer
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read integers into a `std::vector<int>`, then traverse the vector with an iterator-based loop (`begin()`/`end()`) to print every element and compute their sum. Do not use index-based access (`vec[i]`) to walk the vector.

## Input
- Line 1: a single integer `N` (1 <= N <= 20), the number of elements.
- Line 2: exactly `N` space-separated integers (may be negative).

## Output
- Line 1: `Elements:` followed by each element, with a single space before every element (so the line reads `Elements: a b c ...` with no trailing space).
- Line 2: `Sum: S` where `S` is the sum of all elements.

test_cases.label: Hidden -- includes zeros, sums to zero

title: Iterator-Based Maximum Finder
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read 6 integers into a `std::array<int, 6>`, then use iterators to find the maximum value and the zero-based index of its **first** occurrence. Do not use index-based access or any standard library algorithm to find the maximum -- walk the array with iterators.

## Input
- A single line containing exactly 6 space-separated integers (may be negative).

## Output
- Line 1: `Max: M` where `M` is the largest element.
- Line 2: `Position: P` where `P` is the zero-based index of the first occurrence of `M`.

## Hint
The distance between two iterators can be obtained by subtracting them: `it - arr.begin()`.

test_cases.label: Hidden -- duplicate max keeps first position
